source_jobs.title: Open Application
source_jobs.description:
Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and addressing the problem of rising compute costs and energy bottlenecks. Sygaldry AI servers combine multiple qubit types within a single, fault-tolerant architecture to deliver the combination of cost, scale, and speed necessary for advanced AI applications. We pioneer new domains in physics, engineering, and AI, tackling the hardest challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture. We're always looking for individuals ready to define the intersection of quantum and AI and drive its profound global impact.

Don’t See the Right Role Currently Posted?

We know great people don’t always fit neatly into a job description. If you’re excited about what we’re building but don’t see a current opening that matches your background, we’d still love to hear from you.

Who We’re Looking For

In addition to technical experience, we’re interested in people who dream big and execute small, who celebrate curiosity and forge their own path. We believe kind people build strong teams!

What to Tell Us

When you apply, please include:

 - A brief introduction about yourself

 - What excites you about Sygaldry

 - The type of work or role you're interested in

 - Links to relevant work, if applicable

 - Whether you could be available to work in our Ann Arbor or San Francisco office

What Happens Next

We review open applications on a rolling basis. If there’s a potential fit—either now or in the future—we’ll reach out to start a conversation.

Why This Matters

Your work accelerates the path to quantum superintelligence. Each quantum component integrated, each AI model enhanced, each instruction set optimized brings us closer to a future where intelligence and quantum mechanics are inextricably intertwined. We're not building incremental improvements - we're creating exponential transformations that will make AI more affordable, sustainable, personalized, and fundamentally more capable.

How We’re Different

At Sygaldry, curiosity and intellectual courage drive our work. We approach ambitious challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture and know that kind people build the strongest teams. We prioritize mission over ego and collaborate openly with a strong sense of shared purpose. We dream big, yet we execute with a love of detail. We’re looking for scientists, engineers, and operators to forge new paths with us at the intersection of quantum and AI.

Culture & Benefits

 - Visa Sponsorship - We know what it takes to make top talent thrive here. We’re open to supporting visas whenever possible.

 - Compensation - We value your contribution and invest in your future with a competitive salary and meaningful equity.

 - Benefits - Your well-being matters. We provide company-sponsored health coverage to give you and your family peace of mind.

 - Connection - Whether it’s company offsite or casual crew socials, we make time to connect, recharge, and have fun together.

 - Time Off - We trust you to take the time you need. Unlimited PTO so you can rest, recharge, and come back ready to make an impact.

We encourage applications from candidates with diverse backgrounds. We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristics.

source_jobs.title: Head of Communications
source_jobs.description:
About Sygaldry

Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and addressing the problem of rising compute costs and energy bottlenecks. Sygaldry AI servers combine multiple qubit types within a single, fault-tolerant architecture to deliver the combination of cost, scale, and speed necessary for advanced AI applications. We pioneer new domains in physics, engineering, and AI, tackling the hardest challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture. We're looking for individuals ready to define the intersection of quantum and AI and drive its profound global impact.

About the Role

We're hiring our first Head of Communications to define and build Sygaldry's communications function from the ground up. This is a hands-on leadership role: you will personally craft our brand narrative, write and pitch stories to the media, lead our website build, coach executives on communications, and set the strategic direction for how Sygaldry shows up in the world.

You'll work closely with the founding team, including our CEO, Chad Rigetti, to translate pioneering technical work into narratives that resonate with scientific, technical, and mainstream audiences alike. You'll manage external agency partners and lay the foundation to scale the function as the company grows.

The person who takes this role will shape how Sygaldry is perceived from the beginning. That's a rare opportunity, and we're looking for someone who recognizes it.

What You’ll Own

 - Communications Strategy: Define and own Sygaldry’s communications roadmap, setting priorities that support thought leadership, product milestones, and market positioning. Identify the moments that matter most for the company and build campaigns around them. Measure what’s working and iterate as the company evolves.

 - Brand & Narrative: Build Sygaldry’s core messaging framework, brand voice, and positioning, including translating technical concepts. Ensure consistency across every touchpoint, from website copy to decks to social presence.

 - Media Relations: Foster relationships with key journalists and outlets. Develop and execute press strategy, write press releases and announcements, and manage communications.

 - Website & Digital Presence: Work with agencies to own the company website end-to-end, from content strategy and copywriting to project-managing design and development through launch. Keep the site current as the company evolves, ensuring it communicates Sygaldry’s mission, technology, and culture effectively to every audience.

 - Executive Communications: Serve as a strategic communications advisor to Sygaldry leaders. Prepare executives for media interviews, conference panels, keynotes, and investor presentations, including talking points, messaging guidance, and presentation coaching. Help drive the cadence and clarity of internal communications as the company scales.

 - Event support: Spearhead efforts to amplify Sygaldry’s presence at conferences, events, and recruiting engagements. Secure speaking opportunities for executives, coordinate company-hosted events, and support asset creation.

You May Be a Good Fit If You

We're looking for a senior communications professional with at least 10 years of experience, including time at startups or in technical organizations. Beyond that, a few things matter most:

 - You've built or significantly shaped a communications function, not just executed within one. You know what it looks like to start from scratch.

 - You're a strong writer who personally drafts the work. Press releases, website copy, executive talking points, blog posts – you do this yourself, not just review others' drafts.

 - You can translate complex technical material into narratives that land with scientists, engineers, and general audiences. You won't need us to dumb things down for you.

 - You h

source_jobs.title: Head of System Architecture, Frontier AI Hardware
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SYGALDRY

Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and addressing the problem of rising compute costs and energy bottlenecks. Sygaldry AI servers combine multiple qubit types within a single, fault-tolerant architecture to deliver the combination of cost, scale, and speed necessary for advanced AI applications. We pioneer new domains in physics, engineering, and AI, tackling the hardest challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture. We're looking for individuals ready to define the intersection of quantum and AI and drive its profound global impact.




ABOUT THE ROLE:

This role is focused on the architecting of high-performing AI hardware, and does not require prior knowledge of quantum computers. This is a hands-on architecture role for someone who enjoys moving between whiteboard-level system design, performance modeling, and lightweight simulation/emulation, with direct influence on hardware, firmware, and software co-design decisions. We will share more detail about this role during in-person discussions.




YOU MAY BE A GOOD FIT IF YOU HAVE:

 - Architected end-to-end heterogeneous compute systems spanning CPUs, GPUs and novel accelerators.

 - Analyzed scale-up interconnects (e.g., NVLink, UALink, Infinity Fabric, or equivalent) and their impact on latency, bandwidth, and synchronization of high performance AI hardware

 - Designed timing arcs, critical paths, and throughput models across complex multi-device systems.

 - Identified system-level bottlenecks in training, inference, and reinforcement learning workflows at hyperscaler scale.

 - Modeled and reasoned about GPU collective operations (e.g., NCCL, RCCL, or equivalents) across nodes, racks, and pods.

 - Evaluated how collective communication patterns interact with emerging accelerators and non-GPU compute elements.

 - Worked with software and firmware teams to ensure architectural assumptions map cleanly to real-world implementations.




WHY THIS MATTERS

Your work accelerates the path to quantum superintelligence. Each quantum component integrated, each AI model enhanced, each instruction set optimized brings us closer to a future where intelligence and quantum mechanics are inextricably intertwined. We're not building incremental improvements - we're creating exponential transformations that will make AI more affordable, sustainable, personalized, and fundamentally more capable.




HOW WE’RE DIFFERENT

At Sygaldry, curiosity and intellectual courage drive our work. We approach ambitious challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture and know that kind people build the strongest teams. We prioritize mission over ego and collaborate openly with a strong sense of shared purpose. We dream big, yet we execute with a love of detail. We’re looking for scientists, engineers, and operators to forge new paths with us at the intersection of quantum and AI.




CULTURE & BENEFITS

 - Visa Sponsorship - We know what it takes to make top talent thrive here. We’re open to supporting visas whenever possible.

 - Compensation - We value your contribution and invest in your future with a competitive salary and meaningful equity.

 - Benefits - Your well-being matters. We provide company-sponsored health coverage to give you and your family peace of mind.

 - Connection - Whether it’s company offsite or casual crew socials, we make time to connect, recharge, and have fun together.

 - Time Off - We trust you to take the time you need. Unlimited PTO so you can rest, recharge, and come back ready to make an impact.

We encourage applications from candidates with diverse backgrounds. We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, o

source_jobs.title: Lab Manager - Quantum Hardware
source_jobs.description:
About Sygaldry

Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and addressing the problem of rising compute costs and energy bottlenecks. Sygaldry AI servers combine multiple qubit types within a single, fault-tolerant architecture to deliver the combination of cost, scale, and speed necessary for advanced AI applications. We pioneer new domains in physics, engineering, and AI, tackling the hardest challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture. We're looking for individuals ready to define the intersection of quantum and AI and drive its profound global impact.



About the Role

We are seeking a Lab Manager to oversee the day-to-day operations of an experimental research laboratory. This role is responsible for ensuring that the lab runs safely, efficiently, and reliably. You will serve as the connective tissue across equipment, facilities, vendors, and personnel—owning everything from procurement and compliance to lab organization and infrastructure. This is a hands-on role that combines operational excellence with technical awareness in a highly dynamic environment.



What You'll Work On

 - Manage day-to-day lab operations, ensuring a safe, organized, and productive environment

 - Manage procurement of lab equipment, components, and consumables, including vendor coordination and inventory tracking

 - Maintain and oversee lab infrastructure: benches, utilities, gas systems, vacuum systems, and general workspace organization

 - Coordinate installation, maintenance, and repair of laboratory equipment

 - Support researchers with lab setup needs, including space allocation and experiment readiness

 - Develop and enforce lab safety procedures, documentation, and training

 - Ensure compliance with relevant regulations (e.g., safety, environmental, institutional requirements)

 - Track and manage shared resources, ensuring availability and uptime of critical equipment

 - Improve operational processes to increase efficiency and reduce friction in experimental workflows

 - Assist with onboarding new team members into the lab environment



You May Be a Good Fit If You Have

 - Experience managing or supporting operations in a laboratory, engineering, or technical environment

 - Strong organizational skills and attention to detail, with the ability to manage many parallel tasks

 - Experience with procurement, vendor management, and inventory systems

 - Familiarity with laboratory infrastructure (e.g., vacuum systems, gas handling, electronics benches, or similar)

 - Working knowledge of lab safety practices and regulatory compliance

 - Ability to troubleshoot and coordinate solutions for equipment and facility issues

 - Strong communication skills and a collaborative mindset across diverse teams (scientists, engineers, operations)

 - A proactive, ownership-driven approach—you identify problems and solve them before they escalate



How We’re Different

At Sygaldry, curiosity and intellectual courage drive our work. We approach ambitious challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture and know that kind people build the strongest teams. We prioritize mission over ego and collaborate openly with a strong sense of shared purpose. We dream big, yet we execute with a love of detail. We’re looking for scientists, engineers, and operators to forge new paths with us at the intersection of quantum and AI.

source_jobs.title: Senior Quantum Architect
source_jobs.description:
About Sygaldry

Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and addressing the problem of rising compute costs and energy bottlenecks. Sygaldry AI servers combine multiple qubit types within a single, fault-tolerant architecture to deliver the combination of cost, scale, and speed necessary for advanced AI applications. We pioneer new domains in physics, engineering, and AI, tackling the hardest challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture. We're looking for individuals ready to define the intersection of quantum and AI and drive its profound global impact.



About You:

Sygaldry Technologies is seeking an exceptional scientist to serve as a Senior Quantum Architect. In this role, you will own the architectural vision for integrating multiple complementary qubit modalities into a fault-tolerant quantum computing system, bridging error correction theory with the realities of physical hardware. This is a rare opportunity to shape the foundational architecture of a new quantum computing paradigm.



What You'll Work On:

 - Define and drive the end-to-end fault-tolerant architecture for novel hardware spanning multiple qubit modalities, from QEC theory down to hardware-level implementation

 - Make and defend high-stakes architectural trade-offs across QEC codes and fault-tolerant logic under real device constraints and noise

 - Translate cutting-edge error correction theory into hardware-aware protocols validated on physical devices

 - Co-design the architecture with target applications in mind, tailoring fault-tolerant resource allocation and logical operations to quantum algorithms for AI and machine learning workloads

 - Establish the simulation, modeling, and benchmarking frameworks used to guide system design and de-risk the technical roadmap

 - Set technical direction and mentor a multidisciplinary team of experimental and theoretical physicists, engineers, and computer scientists
   
   

You May Be a Good Fit If You Have:

 - A PhD in physics, applied physics, computer science, electrical engineering, or an equivalent degree in a similar field

 - 8+ years of experience in quantum error correction and fault-tolerant architecture, including senior or lead roles

 - A strong publication record in quantum error correction, fault tolerance, or related fields

 - Demonstrated experience implementing and validating QEC or fault-tolerant protocols on real quantum hardware

 - Hands-on experience taking quantum technology from theory through deployment, ideally in an industry setting

 - Strong programming skills in a scientific computing environment

 - A proven track record of leading successful cross-team, multidisciplinary projects

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, including the ability to communicate architectural decisions to both technical and executive audiences
   
   

How We’re Different

At Sygaldry, curiosity and intellectual courage drive our work. We approach ambitious challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture and know that kind people build the strongest teams. We prioritize mission over ego and collaborate openly with a strong sense of shared purpose. We dream big, yet we execute with a love of detail. We’re looking for scientists, engineers, and operators to forge new paths with us at the intersection of quantum and AI.



Culture & Benefits

 - Benefits - Your well-being matters. We provide company-sponsored health coverage to give you and your family peace of mind.

 - Connection - Whether it’s company offsite or casual crew socials, we make time to

source_jobs.description:

Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and addressing the problem of rising compute costs and energy bottlenecks. THE ROLE

Sygaldry is seeking an exceptional Business Development and Partnerships leader. This role is responsible for identifying, developing, and executing strategic growth opportunities that expand Sygaldry's market presence and establish foundational customer and partner relationships that will underpin commercial adoption of our quantum-accelerated AI servers.

The successful candidate will cultivate and deepen relationships with prospective anchor customers and partners. Working closely with leadership and technical teams, this individual will lead complex engagements from initial contact through structured commitment deal execution.




WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

 - Manage strategic accounts, driving the relationship and commercial trajectory over time and expand engagement as the partnership matures.

 - Build trusted relationships with technical and business leaders, serving as a credible counterpart to senior stakeholders.

 - Identify leads, conduct outreach, and secure customer contracts. Structure and negotiate complex, high-value commercial agreements, often where no template exists.

 - Partner closely with technical teams to translate Sygaldry's roadmap and milestones into clear business value for customers, and feed customer needs back into product and engineering

 - Represent Sygaldry at industry conferences and events
   
   


YOU MAY BE A GOOD FIT IF YOU HAVE:

 - 10+ years of business development, strategic partnerships, or related experience within technology industries.

 - A proven track record of generating revenue and closing complex strategic partnerships.

 - Experience engaging senior decision makers, with excellent negotiation, communication, and relationship management skills.

 - Operated as an individual builder in ambiguous environments, creating the processes, systems, and playbooks needed to support growth.

 - Thrive in ambiguity, enjoy building from first principles, and are equally comfortable shaping strategy and executing directly.

 - Bachelor's degree required; advanced technical or business degree preferred.

source_jobs.title: Experimental Quantum Physicist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SYGALDRY

Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and addressing the problem of rising compute costs and energy bottlenecks. Sygaldry AI servers combine multiple qubit types within a single, fault-tolerant architecture to deliver the combination of cost, scale, and speed necessary for advanced AI applications. We pioneer new domains in physics, engineering, and AI, tackling the hardest challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture. We're looking for individuals ready to define the intersection of quantum and AI and drive its profound global impact.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We are seeking an experimental physicist with strong laboratory experience in atomic, optical, or quantum systems. You will play a key role in building and operating cutting-edge experimental platforms for qubit control, measurement, and scaling. The ideal candidate enjoys hands-on work, creative problem-solving, and collaborating across disciplines to make new quantum technologies a reality.




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

 - Design, construct, and characterize precision optical and/or cryogenic systems for quantum experiments.

 - Understand and implement protocols for qubit preparation, control, and readout.

 - Integrate control electronics, laser or rf and microwave systems, and data acquisition.

 - Analyze experimental results and optimize system performance.

 - Cross-team collaboration to guide system design and development.
   
   


YOU MAY BE A GOOD FIT IF YOU HAVE

 - Ph.D. in Physics, Applied Physics, Electrical Engineering or a related field.

 - Hands-on experience working with advanced laboratory instrumentation and quantum systems including AMO physics, solid-state and/or superconducting systems.

 - Familiarity with qubit control, spectroscopy, or precision measurement techniques.

 - Strong background in experimental design, data analysis, and scientific problem solving.

 - Experience with Python or similar tools for experiment automation.

 - Excellent communication skills and a collaborative mindset.




HOW WE’RE DIFFERENT

At Sygaldry, curiosity and intellectual courage drive our work. We approach ambitious challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture and know that kind people build the strongest teams. We prioritize mission over ego and collaborate openly with a strong sense of shared purpose. We dream big, yet we execute with a love of detail. We’re looking for scientists, engineers, and operators to forge new paths with us at the intersection of quantum and AI.




CULTURE & BENEFITS

 - Visa Sponsorship - We know what it takes to make top talent thrive here. We’re open to supporting visas whenever possible.

 - Compensation - We value your contribution and invest in your future with a competitive salary and meaningful equity.

 - Benefits - Your well-being matters. We provide company-sponsored health coverage to give you and your family peace of mind.

 - Connection - Whether it’s company offsite or casual crew socials, we make time to connect, recharge, and have fun together.

 - Time Off - We trust you to take the time you need. Unlimited PTO so you can rest, recharge, and come back ready to make an impact.
   
   
   

We encourage applications from candidates with diverse backgrounds. We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic.

We encourage you to apply even if you do not believe you meet every single qualification. If you don’t think this role is right for you, but you believe that you would have something meaningful to contribute to our mission, please reach out at letsbuild@sygaldry.com

source_jobs.title: Head of Modeling & Simulation, Frontier AI Hardware
source_jobs.description:
About Sygaldry

Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and engineering the conditions for it to scale efficiently and operate affordably. Sygaldry AI servers combine multiple qubit types within a single, fault-tolerant architecture to deliver the combination of cost, scale, and speed necessary for advanced AI applications. We pioneer new domains in physics, engineering, and AI, tackling the hardest challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture. We're looking for individuals ready to define the intersection of quantum and AI and drive its profound global impact.



What You’ll Work On

This role is focused on the modeling and simulation of high-performing AI hardware, and does not require prior knowledge of quantum computers. This role is empowered to lead the definition and implementation of the simulation, modeling and emulation roadmap for the company. It will interface with a variety of internal teams and external partners with whom we are planning to collaborate. We will share more detail about this role during in-person discussions.

 

Key responsibilities:

 - Lead the effort to design, select and implement Sygaldry's distributed simulation environment

 - Define the simulation, modeling and emulation roadmap for the company

 - Set everything up the simulation infrastructure from scratch

 - Interface with a variety of internal teams and external collaboration partners

 - Work with internal teams that have developed estimation tools to coach them and help them integrate these tools into the simulation & modeling framework



You May Be a Fit if You Have:

 - Lead the effort to design, select and implement the modeling and simulation environment of a distributed AI acceleration architecture

 - Modeled complex training, inference and reasoning workflows implemented in a heterogeneous AI cluster of GPUs, CPUs and novel accelerators

 - Exposure to the simulation environments used by the GPU/XPU supplier, hyperscalers and/or frontier labs for simulation AI workloads on various system and hardware architectures

 - Familiarity with architecture modeling and distributed simulation platforms, such as Gem5, ASTRA-sim and such

 - Experience with C or SystemC behavioral models and timing-aware behavioral models

 - Experience with Verilog/VHDL RTL simulation platforms like VCS



How We’re Different

We’re building the infrastructure for quantum superintelligence and pioneering new domains at the intersection of physics, engineering, and AI. At Sygaldry, curiosity and intellectual courage drive our work. We approach ambitious challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture and know that kind people build the strongest teams. We prioritize mission over ego and collaborate openly with a strong sense of shared purpose. We dream big, yet we execute with a love of detail.



Culture & Benefits

 - The Best of Both Worlds: Access to the industrial-scale compute and hardware resources while retaining the intellectual freedom of an academic environment.

 - Visa Sponsorship: We know what it takes to make top talent thrive here. We're open to supporting visas whenever possible.

 - Compensation: We value your high-level expertise. This position offers a salary significantly above standard academic postdoctoral rates.

 - Benefits: Your well-being matters. We provide company-sponsored health coverage to give you and your family peace of mind.

 - Connection: Whether it’s company offsite or casual crew socials, we make time to connect, recharge, and have fun together.

 - Time Off: We trust you to take the time you need. Unlimited PTO so you can rest, recharge, and come back ready to make an impact.

This position can work out of our San Francisco or Ann Arbor offices. Competitive compensation package is based on experience and location. Expected b

source_jobs.title: Research Scientist - Frontier AI/ML & Quantum Algorithms
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SYGALDRY

Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and addressing the problem of rising compute costs and energy bottlenecks. Sygaldry AI servers combine multiple qubit types within a single, fault-tolerant architecture to deliver the combination of cost, scale, and speed necessary for advanced AI applications. We pioneer new domains in physics, engineering, and AI, tackling the hardest challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture. We're looking for individuals ready to define the intersection of quantum and AI and drive its profound global impact.




ABOUT THE ROLE

Frontier AI is moving toward scientific reasoning and design: molecules, materials, proteins, weather, climate, dynamical systems, quantum devices, and controlled physical systems. These domains expose deep computational bottlenecks in sampling, probabilistic inference, optimization, simulation, uncertainty quantification, inverse design, planning, and control.

Sygaldry is building quantum-accelerated AI systems for this next era. We are looking for a Research Scientist who can help define Quantum AI: not just quantum machine learning, but the broader study of how fault-tolerant quantum computation can transform the primitives of learning, inference, reasoning, prediction, geometry, and control.

In this role, you will work at the intersection of frontier AI/ML, quantum algorithms, scientific machine learning, and hardware-software co-design. You will identify where quantum computation can provide genuine structural advantage for AI workloads, develop new theoretical and empirical frameworks, and translate research insights into systems that inform real quantum hardware and AI architecture decisions.

 


WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON

Frontier AI for Scientific Discovery

Develop and study models for high-dimensional scientific prediction, generation, and design, including:

 - Diffusion models, flow matching, consistency models, score-based generative models, energy-based models, latent-variable models, autoregressive models, and normalizing flows.

 - Scientific foundation models for molecules, materials, proteins, quantum systems, weather, climate, PDEs, and dynamical systems.

 - Graph neural networks, geometric deep learning, equivariant models, neural operators, tensor methods, manifold learning, and learning on structured state spaces.

 - Models that combine prediction, uncertainty, active learning, and closed-loop design for scientific discovery.

Learning, Inference, and Reasoning

Build algorithms and theory for the computational primitives that matter most for next-generation AI systems:

 - Probabilistic inference, Bayesian modeling, variational inference, Monte Carlo methods, simulation-based inference, uncertainty quantification, and calibration.

 - Optimization, sampling, amortized inference, sequential decision-making, Bayesian experimental design, reinforcement learning, planning, and control.

 - Scientific reasoning systems, model-guided discovery, algorithmic discovery, and agents that can propose, test, and refine hypotheses.

 - Benchmarking frameworks that reveal when a new computational substrate changes scaling behavior, not just constant factors.

Quantum Algorithms for AI Workloads

Identify where quantum computation can accelerate or reshape AI-relevant subroutines, including:

 - Quantum algorithms for sampling, integration, Monte Carlo acceleration, linear algebra, optimization, Hamiltonian simulation, quantum simulation, and tensor-structured computation.

 - Fault-tolerant quantum algorithms, resource estimation, complexity analysis, block encoding, QSVT, LCU methods, amplitude estimation, phase estimation, and quantum walks.

 - Hybrid quantum-classical workflows where quantum primitives are embedded inside classical AI pipelines.

 - New quantum-native mo

source_jobs.title: Quantum Error Correction Theorist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SYGALDRY

Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and addressing the problem of rising compute costs and energy bottlenecks. Sygaldry AI servers combine multiple qubit types within a single, fault-tolerant architecture to deliver the combination of cost, scale, and speed necessary for advanced AI applications. We pioneer new domains in physics, engineering, and AI, tackling the hardest challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture. We're looking for individuals ready to define the intersection of quantum and AI and drive its profound global impact.




ABOUT YOU:

Sygaldry Technologies is seeking an experienced scientist to serve as a Senior Quantum Error Correction Theorist. In this role, you will be responsible for developing quantum error correction schemes to integrate multiple complementary qubit modalities into a fault-tolerant architecture. This is a rare opportunity to join an early team and help lay the foundation for a new quantum computing paradigm.




ABOUT THE ROLE




WHAT YOU'LL WORK ON:

 - Design and evaluate quantum error correction schemes for novel hardware architectures with multiple qubit modalities

 - Develop efficient protocols for fault-tolerance under hardware-specific constraints and noise models

 - Build simulation and modeling tools for system design and analysis

 - Lead high-impact research projects with a multidisciplinary team of experimental and theoretical physicists, engineers, and computer scientists




YOU MAY BE A GOOD FIT IF YOU HAVE:

 - A PhD in physics, applied physics, computer science, electrical engineering, or an equivalent degree in a similar field

 - 5-10 years of experience in quantum error correction

 - Publications in quantum error correction or related fields

 - Possess a strong programming skills in a scientific computing environment

 - Proven track record of successful cross-team collaborative projects

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills



Strong Candidates May Have:

 - Experience translating cutting-edge R&D into commercial products or roadmap milestones

 - Experience deploying quantum error correction schemes on real quantum devices

 - Experience translating abstract models of error correction into hardware-aware protocols

 - Experience with decoding





HOW WE’RE DIFFERENT

At Sygaldry, curiosity and intellectual courage drive our work. We approach ambitious challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture and know that kind people build the strongest teams. We prioritize mission over ego and collaborate openly with a strong sense of shared purpose. We dream big, yet we execute with a love of detail. We’re looking for scientists, engineers, and operators to forge new paths with us at the intersection of quantum and AI.




CULTURE & BENEFITS

 - Visa Sponsorship - We know what it takes to make top talent thrive here. We’re open to supporting visas whenever possible.

 - Compensation - We value your contribution and invest in your future with a competitive salary and meaningful equity.

 - Benefits - Your well-being matters. We provide company-sponsored health coverage to give you and your family peace of mind.

 - Connection - Whether it’s company offsite or casual crew socials, we make time to connect, recharge, and have fun together.

 - Time Off - We trust you to take the time you need. Unlimited PTO so you can rest, recharge, and come back ready to make an impact.




We encourage applications from candidates with diverse backgrounds. We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic.

source_jobs.title: Quantum Algorithm Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE:

In this role, you will help bridge the gap between quantum algorithms and real-world quantum hardware by developing efficient, hardware-ready quantum software solutions. You’ll work on implementing and optimizing quantum circuits for execution across target architectures, while building and maintaining the tools and frameworks that support circuit compilation, transpilation, and deployment. Partnering closely with research and hardware teams, you will profile and benchmark circuit performance, identify optimization opportunities, and contribute to the advancement of internal quantum software infrastructure that enables scalable and high-performance quantum computing workflows.


WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON:

 - Implement and optimize quantum circuits for efficient execution on target hardware architectures

 - Develop and maintain software tools and frameworks for compiling/transpiling, and deploying quantum circuits

 - Profile and benchmark circuit performance, identifying bottlenecks and driving improvements

 - Work closely with research and hardware teams to translate algorithmic designs into hardware-ready implementations

 - Contribute to the development of internal quantum software infrastructure and tooling


YOU MAY BE A GOOD FIT IF YOU HAVE:

 - An MS in physics, computer science, mathematics or a related field

 - Strong software engineering skills, with experience in scientific computing environments (Python, C++, or similar)

 - Hands-on experience with quantum circuit design and quantum software frameworks (e.g. Qiskit, PennyLane, Cirq, or equivalent)

 - Understanding of quantum gate sets, circuit compilation, and hardware constraints

 - Proven ability to deliver robust, maintainable code in a collaborative team environment

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills

 - Strong Candidates May Also Have:

 - Knowledge of quantum error correction codes and fault-tolerant circuit synthesis

 - Experience with noise modeling, error mitigation techniques, or hardware-aware compilation

 - Familiarity with classical HPC or distributed computing as applied to quantum simulation

 - Track record of contributions to open-source quantum software projects


HOW WE’RE DIFFERENT

At Sygaldry, curiosity and intellectual courage drive our work. We approach ambitious challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture and know that kind people build the strongest teams. We prioritize mission over ego and collaborate openly with a strong sense of shared purpose. We dream big, yet we execute with a love of detail. We’re looking for scientists, engineers, and operators to forge new paths with us at the intersection of quantum and AI.




CULTURE & BENEFITS

 - Visa Sponsorship - We know what it takes to make top talent thrive here. We’re open to supporting visas whenever possible.

 - Compensation - We value your contribution and invest in your future with a competitive salary and meaningful equity.

 - Benefits - Your well-being matters. We provide company-sponsored health coverage to give you and your family peace of mind.

 - Connection - Whether it’s company offsite or casual crew socials, we make time to connect, recharge, and have fun together.

 - Time Off - We trust you to take the time you need. Unlimited PTO so you can rest, recharge, and come back ready to make an impact.

We encourage applications from candidates with diverse backgrounds. We are an equal opportunity employer, and we do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, age, veteran status, disability, genetic information, or other applicable legally protected characteristic.

We encourage you to apply even if you do not believe you meet every single qualification. If you don’t think this role is right for you, but you believe that you would have something meaningful to to contribute to our mission, please reach out at letsbuild@sygaldry.com.



Location: Ann Arbor, MI (On-Site

source_jobs.title: Technology Partnerships Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SYGALDRY

Sygaldry Technologies is building quantum-accelerated AI servers to exponentially speed up training and inference for AI. By integrating quantum and AI, we're accelerating the path to superintelligence, and addressing the problem of rising compute costs and energy bottlenecks. Sygaldry AI servers combine multiple qubit types within a single, fault-tolerant architecture to deliver the combination of cost, scale, and speed necessary for advanced AI applications. We pioneer new domains in physics, engineering, and AI, tackling the hardest challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture. We're looking for individuals ready to define the intersection of quantum and AI and drive its profound global impact.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Technology Partnerships Manager to play a leading role at the intersection of GTM, Product, and AI. You’ll be pivotal in bringing our technology to market by translating our product benefit to end-users, while deeply understanding what customers actually need and influencing our product direction.



You’ll play a key role in landing new customer engagements and working closely with partners. You will help define and run the programs that move customer relationships forward, articulating the milestones that progress commercial engagements.



In this role, you’ll be the connective tissue back into the company from end-users, and work closely with GTM, Product, and AI. If you think like a founder, are excited by autonomy, and love engaging directly with end-users, this role may be a good fit for you.




WHAT YOU’LL WORK ON

You are excited to be at the forefront of shaping how quantum is used to accelerate AI in practice. You’ll identify targets and be in the room from the very beginning for conversations with AI companies and enterprises, including first meetings, deep-dives, and standing working sessions. While you do not need to be an AI expert or have built and trained large models yourself, you do need to be technically experienced, able to understand AI researcher bottlenecks, speak credibly about model architectures, training and inference workloads, evals, and where our algorithms and hardware benefits end users.



You will have deep involvement and leadership in visioning, planning, and execution of critical growth programs. You will manage a portfolio of accounts and each active opportunity will have a program you own to advance the customer relationship, including delivering business and technical proof points. You are key to making cutting-edge deals happen by driving the plan and the credibility behind it, engaging deeply with technical stakeholders, and establishing a reliable and visible cadence for program reviews, decision-making, prioritization, and resource allocation.



As one of the first hires in this function, what you build will outlast any specific deal. Over your first year you’ll codify how this work happens here, so the function can scale.




YOU MAY BE A GOOD FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 7+ years of experience, including as a founder, technical program manager, AI research, product manager, or equivalent at a technology company.

 - Degree in a technical field of study

 - You’ve interfaced with customers and/or partners to help close deals, led programs with external counterparts, and/or managed multi-stakeholder technical projects over long durations.

 - Have strong AI/ML literacy, ability to read recent papers, lead a technical conversation about training and inference, and reason about evals and benchmarks. Researchers will need to take you seriously fairly quickly as a technical go-to-market stakeholder.

 - You are a translator and communicator and excited by engaging with customers.
   
   


HOW WE’RE DIFFERENT

At Sygaldry, curiosity and intellectual courage drive our work. We approach ambitious challenges with a grounded, optimistic, and rigorous culture and know that kind people build the strongest teams. We prioritize mission over ego an
